verses.1: For the music director. A Psalm of David. God of my praise, Do not be silent!
verses.2: For they have opened a wicked and deceitful mouth against me; They have spoken against me with a lying tongue.
verses.3: They have also surrounded me with words of hatred, And have fought against me without cause.
verses.4: In return for my love they act as my accusers; But I am in prayer.
verses.5: So they have repaid me evil for good, And hatred for my love.
verses.6: Appoint a wicked person over him, And may an accuser stand at his right hand.
verses.7: When he is judged, may he come out guilty, And may his prayer become sin.
verses.8: May his days be few; May another take his office.
verses.9: May his children be fatherless, And his wife a widow.
verses.10: May his children wander about and beg; And may they seek sustenance far from their ruined homes.
verses.11: May the creditor seize everything that he has, And may strangers plunder the product of his labor.
verses.12: May there be none to extend kindness to him, Nor any to be gracious to his fatherless children.
verses.13: May his descendants be eliminated; May their name be wiped out in a following generation.
verses.14: May the guilt of his fathers be remembered before the LORD, And do not let the sin of his mother be wiped out.
verses.15: May they be before the LORD continually, So that He may eliminate their memory from the earth;
verses.16: Because he did not remember to show mercy, But persecuted the afflicted and needy person, And the despondent in heart, to put them to death.
verses.17: He also loved cursing, so it came to him; And he did not delight in blessing, so it was far from him.
verses.18: But he clothed himself with cursing as with his garment, And it entered his body like water, And like oil into his bones.
verses.19: May it be to him as a garment with which he covers himself, And as a belt which he constantly wears around himself.
verses.20: May this be the reward of my accusers from the LORD, And of those who speak evil against my soul.
verses.21: But You, GOD, the Lord, deal kindly with me for the sake of Your name; Because Your mercy is good, rescue me;
verses.22: For I am afflicted and needy, And my heart is wounded within me.
verses.23: I am passing like a shadow when it lengthens; I am shaken off like the locust.
verses.24: My knees are weak from fasting, And my flesh has grown lean, without fatness.
verses.25: I also have become a disgrace to them; When they see me, they shake their head.
verses.26: Help me, LORD my God; Save me according to Your mercy.
verses.27: And may they know that this is Your hand; You, LORD, have done it.
verses.28: They will curse, but You bless; When they arise, they will be ashamed, But Your servant will be glad.
verses.29: May my accusers be clothed with dishonor, And may they cover themselves with their own shame as with a robe.
verses.30: With my mouth I will give thanks abundantly to the LORD; And I will praise Him in the midst of many.
verses.31: For He stands at the right hand of the needy, To save him from those who judge his soul.
